# UC-12.3.9 · SBOM Generation Compliance

## Description

CycloneDX/SPDX missing for release builds breaks supply-chain attestation requirements (EO 14028).

## Value

CycloneDX/SPDX missing for release builds breaks supply-chain attestation requirements (EO 14028).

## Implementation

Fail release stage if SBOM not uploaded to blob store. Track SBOM format version (CycloneDX 1.5).

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Syft, build pipeline attestations.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sbom_generated=true`, artifact `sbom_path`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Fail release stage if SBOM not uploaded to blob store. Track SBOM format version (CycloneDX 1.5).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="release:build"
| where sbom_present="false" AND environment="release"
| table _time, application, version, build_id
| sort -_time
```

Understanding this SPL

**SBOM Generation Compliance** — CycloneDX/SPDX missing for release builds breaks supply-chain attestation requirements (EO 14028).

Documented **Data sources**: `sbom_generated=true`, artifact `sbom_path`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Syft, build pipeline attestations. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cicd; **sourcetype**: release:build. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cicd, sourcetype="release:build". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where sbom_present="false" AND environment="release"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **SBOM Generation Compliance**): table _time, application, version, build_id
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the vendor console or native job history for that tool. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (missing SBOM), Single value (compliance %), Line chart (trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=cicd sourcetype="release:build"
| where sbom_present="false" AND environment="release"
| table _time, application, version, build_id
| sort -_time
```
